revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Akasakaimai Tumuli]][[ja:赤坂今井墳墓]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Akasakaimai Tumuli | native_name =赤坂今井墳墓 | native_name_lang = | image = 赤坂今井墳墓 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akasakaimai Tumuli | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Akasakaimai Tumuli | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|38|36.0|N|135|03|02.0|E|region:JP-27|display=inline|qq=}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs ={{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|36.0|N|135|03|02.0|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Akasakaimai Tumuli'''|赤坂今井墳墓||qq=}} is a late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} [[Kofun]], located in the Mineyama neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2007.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/174055|title=赤坂今井墳墓 |trans-title= Akasakaimai Funbo|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Akasakaimai Tumuli is located near the confluence of the Fukuta and Takeno Rivers, on a hill overlooking a narrow valley. It is pyramid-shaped with a trapezoidal layout, and was constructed by the modification of a hill ridge extending from the west. The base of tumulus is 39 meters from north-to-south by 36 meters from east-to-west and has a height of four meters, and the top of the tumulus is 27 meters by 25 meters. Six burials have been confirmed on the flat surface at the top of the mound, and 19 on the flat surface at the foot of the mound (11 on the north side, 3 on the west side, 1 on the south side, and 4 on the east side), of which eleven have been completely [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Of these burials, nine had wooden caskets and four were in earthenware containers. The central burial is believed to be the first burial on the flat surface of the mound, and consisted of a square [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] 14 meters north-south and 10.5 meters east-west. The inside of the casket has not been investigated. On the west side of this grave were four pillar holes in the north and south, which are thought to be associated with the funeral procession. From the {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|qid=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery|1=Yayoi pottery}} found in the excavation, the first burial dates from the end of the late Yayoi period. Pottery from many locations, including the {{ill|Mount Ikoma|en|Mount Ikoma|qid=Q996259|lt=Ikoma Mountains|1=Mount Ikoma}} of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi|1=Kawachi Province}}, the lower reaches of {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|qid=Q929682|lt=Sanuki|1=Sanuki Province}} and the [[:en:Tokai region|Tokai region]] of Japanese were also excavated. In addition, from the 4th grave (which was constructed by cutting into the burial chamber of the first burial), a headdress with a pendant earring was recovered. The headdress contained {{ill|Jasper|en|Jasper|qid=Q201175|lt=Jasper|1=Jasper}} tubular and spherical beads, 30 green lead barium glass ''[[magatama]]'', and {{ill|Han purple and Han blue|en|Han purple and Han blue|qid=Q5646726|lt=Han purple and Han blue|1=Han purple and Han blue}} glass beads which had been imported from the Asian mainland were discovered. The casket in this fourth tomb was also coated in vermilion which had been made from {{ill|Cinnabar|en|Cinnabar|qid=Q104614|lt=Cinnabar|1=Cinnabar}} from mines in {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise|1=Ise Province}}. The excavated items were designated as Kyoto Prefecture designated tangible cultural properties in 2010.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The site was discovered during widening work on Kyoto Prefectural Road No. 17 and had long been believed to have been the ruins of a ''yamashiro''-style {{ill|Japanese castle|en|Japanese castle|qid=Q92026|lt=Japanese castle|1=Japanese castle}} constructed as an outlying fortification of Imai Castle from the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}; however, the preliminary survey found that it was actually a huge Kofun from the much earlier Yayoi period. Kyoto Prefecture decided to preserve the site by changing the method of road construction and Mineyama town acquired the site with the aim of developing an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] in 1999. Excavations were conducted from 2000 to 2003.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The site is about ten minutes by car from {{ill|Mineyama Station|en|Mineyama Station|qid=Q6864579|lt=Mineyama Station|1=Mineyama Station}} on the {{ill|Miyazu Line|en|Miyazu Line|qid=Q6884480|lt=Miyazu Line|1=Miyazu Line}} of the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|en|Kyoto Tango Railway|qid=Q19727758|lt=Kyoto Tango Railway|1=Kyoto Tango Railway}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==Gallery== <gallery> Akasakaimai (2).jpg|View from the east Akasakaimai (6).jpg|View from the south Akasakaimai (7).jpg|Northwest terrace </gallery> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|qid=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Akasakaimai funbo|qq=}} *[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/2/3307.html Kyōtango City home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
